examples.description: left knee — full note: structured history, labelled exam manoeuvres, imaging per view, and an assessment + plan synthesized and cited from the captured findings (Grounded Synthesis Mode, v3.2)

transcript.text: Left knee pain. It's been getting worse over the past two months.
The pain is on the inner side of the knee, worse going up and down stairs and with squatting.
I get some morning stiffness with a slight limp that settles, and the knee swells on and off.
I'm very active — swimming three times a week, dancing, and weights.
I take adalimumab 40 milligrams by injection every two weeks for Crohn's, and Tylenol or Advil as needed.
Let me have a look. On inspection there's no gross deformity and no obvious swelling.
Range of motion is full and passive on both knees, with pain only on the left.
There's tenderness on palpation along the medial joint line of the left knee.
McMurray is negative on the right and positive on the left. Lachman is negative and the ligaments are stable.
On the standing AP view there's medial joint space narrowing of the left knee.
On the sunrise view there's a small osteophyte under the patella on the left.
I'll order an MRI of the left knee to look at the cartilage and the meniscus.
I'll refer you to physiotherapy to strengthen the muscles around the knee.
We can talk about a viscosupplementation injection, around four hundred and fifty dollars, which acts as a joint lubricant; the main risk is infection, roughly one in ten thousand.
Keep active — no restriction on squats or lunges — and we'll reassess after the MRI.

claims.text: Left knee pain, worsening over the past two months.
claims.source_quote: Left knee pain. It's been getting worse over the past two months.

claims.text: Pain located at the medial aspect of the left knee, aggravated by ascending and descending stairs and by squatting.
claims.source_quote: The pain is on the inner side of the knee, worse going up and down stairs and with squatting.

claims.text: Morning stiffness with a slight limp that settles, and intermittent swelling of the knee.
claims.source_quote: I get some morning stiffness with a slight limp that settles, and the knee swells on and off.

claims.text: Highly active — swimming three times a week, dancing, and resistance training.
claims.source_quote: I'm very active — swimming three times a week, dancing, and weights.

claims.text: Adalimumab 40 mg subcutaneous injection every two weeks; Tylenol or Advil as needed.
claims.source_quote: I take adalimumab 40 milligrams by injection every two weeks for Crohn's, and Tylenol or Advil as needed.

claims.text: Inspection: no gross deformity and no obvious swelling.
claims.source_quote: On inspection there's no gross deformity and no obvious swelling.

claims.text: Range of motion: full passive range of motion in both knees, with pain only on the left.
claims.source_quote: Range of motion is full and passive on both knees, with pain only on the left.

claims.text: Palpation: tenderness along the medial joint line of the left knee.
claims.source_quote: There's tenderness on palpation along the medial joint line of the left knee.

claims.text: Special tests: McMurray negative on the right and positive on the left; Lachman negative with stable ligaments.
claims.source_quote: McMurray is negative on the right and positive on the left. Lachman is negative and the ligaments are stable.

claims.text: Standing AP view: medial joint space narrowing of the left knee.
claims.source_quote: On the standing AP view there's medial joint space narrowing of the left knee.

claims.text: Sunrise view: small osteophyte under the patella on the left.
claims.source_quote: On the sunrise view there's a small osteophyte under the patella on the left.

claims.text: Working assessment: early medial compartment osteoarthritis of the left knee — medial joint space narrowing on imaging with medial joint-line tenderness on examination.
claims.source_quote: On the standing AP view there's medial joint space narrowing of the left knee.

additional_sources.source_quote: There's tenderness on palpation along the medial joint line of the left knee.

claims.text: Patellofemoral involvement on the left — small retropatellar osteophyte on the sunrise view, with a positive McMurray on the left to be clarified on MRI.
claims.source_quote: On the sunrise view there's a small osteophyte under the patella on the left.

additional_sources.source_quote: McMurray is negative on the right and positive on the left. Lachman is negative and the ligaments are stable.

claims.text: Investigations: MRI of the left knee to assess cartilage and meniscus.
claims.source_quote: I'll order an MRI of the left knee to look at the cartilage and the meniscus.

claims.text: Referrals: physiotherapy to strengthen the musculature around the knee.
claims.source_quote: I'll refer you to physiotherapy to strengthen the muscles around the knee.

claims.text: Options discussed: viscosupplementation injection (~$450) as a joint lubricant; risk of infection counselled at approximately 1 in 10,000.
claims.source_quote: We can talk about a viscosupplementation injection, around four hundred and fifty dollars, which acts as a joint lubricant; the main risk is infection, roughly one in ten thousand.

claims.text: Activity and follow-up: remain active with no restriction on squats or lunges; reassess after the MRI.
claims.source_quote: Keep active — no restriction on squats or lunges — and we'll reassess after the MRI.
